description: Use Codex CLI or OpenCode in Obsidian to manage knowledge, ask vault questions, and review local agent work. - This plugin has not been manually reviewed by Obsidian staff.

releases.author: AKin-lvyifang
releases.description:
![Codex EchoInk 1.0.0 Home Dashboard](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/AKin-lvyifang/codex-echoink/1.0.0/docs/images/codex-echoink-home-dashboard-v1.0.0.png)

## Codex EchoInk 1.0.0

EchoInk now has a dedicated Home dashboard for managing an Obsidian knowledge base.

### New Features

- Home tab: can open by default, can be closed, and can be reopened from the Obsidian command palette.
- Ribbon entry: opens both the EchoInk sidebar and the Home dashboard.
- Knowledge status: shows Wiki health, Raw pending work, yearly check heatmap, calendar, health score, and key vault counts.
- Note cards: shows recent Wiki updates and recommendations in a responsive card stream.
- Filters and sorting: supports status, recommendation group, update time, relevance, and first-level folder.
- Card links: copies Obsidian internal links, relative paths, and Markdown links.

### How to Use

1. Enable Home on startup in EchoInk settings.
2. Open Home from the ribbon icon or the Obsidian command palette.
3. Use the filters above the cards to focus your knowledge base view.
4. Use each card menu to copy the link format you need.

### Manual Install

Download `codex-echoink-1.0.0.zip`, unzip it, and place the `codex-echoink` folder in:

```text
<vault>/.obsidian/plugins/codex-echoink/
```

The plugin folder should contain `main.js`, `manifest.json`, and `styles.css`.
releases.name: 1.0.0 - Home Dashboard

releases.author: AKin-lvyifang
releases.description:
![Codex EchoInk 0.8.0 Knowledge Safety](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/AKin-lvyifang/codex-echoink/main/docs/images/codex-echoink-knowledge-safety-v0.8.0.png)

## Codex EchoInk 0.8.0

Knowledge safety update: Raw sources, local history, and background Codex threads are now handled as separate layers.

### New in this release

- Raw sources are protected more strictly during `/check`, `/maintain`, and `/calibrate raw`.
- Knowledge history stays readable from EchoInk's local history store through `/history`.
- Background Codex threads created by Knowledge commands are archived after task results are saved, reducing recent-list clutter in Codex Desktop.
- Canceled, timed-out, retried, or failed Knowledge runs now leave clearer state in the UI.
- The Knowledge dashboard Raw count now focuses on actual source notes instead of `.assets/` image files.

### How to use

1. Run `/check` for a read-only Knowledge health check.
2. Run `/maintain` to process changed Raw sources.
3. Use `/history` to browse local Knowledge history. Removing Codex archived conversations does not remove plugin history.

<details>
<summary>Download verification</summary>

- `codex-echoink-0.8.0.zip`
- SHA256: `2f4924b8db41b3daf5f45439741e6923c952086d268e691f66197af1d06096e9`

</details>
releases.name: 0.8.0 - Knowledge Safety

releases.author: AKin-lvyifang
releases.description:
![Codex EchoInk 0.7.2](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/AKin-lvyifang/codex-echoink/main/docs/images/codex-echoink-turn-queue-v0.7.2.png)

## v0.7.2

Turn Queue and composer menu stability update for Codex EchoInk.

### What changed

- Clicking inside the composer area but outside Skill and Knowledge command menus now closes open composer menus.
- Clicking a Skill or Knowledge command menu no longer closes that menu by mistake.
- The queue entry UI has regression coverage so follow-up task entry stays stable.

### How to use

1. Open the Codex EchoInk sidebar.
2. Click the Skill button or type a Knowledge command trigger.
3. Use the menu normally; click back into the composer when you want it to close.

<details>
<summary>Download check</summary>

- Asset: codex-echoink-0.7.2.zip
- SHA256: 3e55edc7b31f0b5ae37e82be3ce7bf5fd23602a67d5af0046b1b79ec56be200b

</details>
releases.name: 0.7.2 - Turn Queue Menu Stability

releases.author: AKin-lvyifang
releases.description:
![Codex EchoInk Turn Queue](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/AKin-lvyifang/codex-echoink/main/docs/images/codex-echoink-turn-queue-v0.7.0.png)

## v0.7.1

Turn Queue stability update for Codex EchoInk.

### What changed

- Successful tasks continue the queue only when another item is waiting.
- Failed or stopped tasks pause the queue and keep remaining work for manual resume.
- Queued turns no longer start while an ordinary turn, Knowledge task, or queue startup is already in progress.
- Dragging queue cards stays inside the queue UI instead of leaking into the composer attachment drop area.

### How to use

1. Start a chat or Knowledge command.
2. Type the next task while the current one is running.
3. Click Enqueue.
4. Reorder or delete waiting items above the composer.
5. If a task is stopped or fails, click Resume queue when ready.

<details>
<summary>Download check</summary>

- Asset: codex-echoink-0.7.1.zip
- SHA256: 65639acbd516b42632417fa3683e36fbc12f70f5802a2b9af91302ad6fc20d91

</details>
releases.name: 0.7.1 - Turn Queue Stability

releases.author: AKin-lvyifang
releases.description:
![Codex EchoInk 0.7 Turn Queue](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/AKin-lvyifang/codex-echoink/main/docs/images/codex-echoink-turn-queue-v0.7.0.png)

## Codex EchoInk 0.7.0

Turn Queue for ordinary chat and Knowledge channel work.

### What changed

- Queue follow-up tasks while the current Agent turn is still running.
- The primary button becomes `Enqueue` when a task is running and the composer has text.
- With an empty composer, the primary button still stops the current task.
- Queue items preserve their text, attachments, Skill, model, permission, mode, and workspace.
- Successful tasks automatically run the next queued item.
- Failed or stopped tasks pause the queue and keep the remaining items for manual resume.
- Knowledge commands such as `/ask`, `/maintain`, and `/journal` now run serially.

### How to use

1. Start an ordinary chat or Knowledge command.
2. Type the next task while the current one is running.
3. Click `Enqueue`.
4. Reorder or delete waiting items above the composer.
5. If a task stops or fails, click `Resume queue`.

<details>
<summary>Download verification</summary>

- Asset: `codex-echoink-0.7.0.zip`
- SHA256: `a8b39d62b6c8f6c3d4b6b74ce4e6bbbfa61da9f782eba67ada5e4b0a1f8e94b2`

</details>
releases.name: 0.7.0 - Turn Queue

releases.author: AKin-lvyifang
releases.description:
![Codex EchoInk 0.6.0](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/AKin-lvyifang/codex-echoink/0.6.0/docs/images/codex-echoink-v0.6.0-card.svg)

## Version

`0.6.0`

## New features

- Added a first-run setup guide that checks Codex CLI, Codex login, OpenCode CLI, OpenCode server, models, and Agent readiness.
- Added missing-environment guidance with install commands, copy buttons, and documentation links.
- Added `Run check again` to re-detect CLI paths, refresh Codex login, and reconnect or start OpenCode when needed.
- Added `Start` as an explicit setup completion step. It opens the EchoInk sidebar without sending a message or running a Knowledge task.

## Fixes and maintenance

- Added Windows path detection for Codex CLI and OpenCode CLI.
- Upgraded Knowledge history to day-based archive storage with settings tools for indexing, export, and compaction.
- Tightened Knowledge maintenance so Agent tasks cannot directly rewrite Raw source bodies; raw path normalization is handled by plugin-side checks.
- Improved Knowledge maintenance reports, dashboard state, local note links, and history entry placement.

## How to use

1. Download `codex-echoink-0.6.0.zip`.
2. Unzip it into `<vault>/.obsidian/plugins/codex-echoink/`.
3. Restart Obsidian and enable `Codex EchoInk`.
4. Open plugin settings, follow the setup guide, then click `Start`.

<details>
<summary>Download checksums</summary>

```text
62cfc5dc9be8f292f20636f1770892ebfccaa31cf3e33be9cb6fd4d685de3d59  codex-echoink-0.6.0.zip
ede96fa6af225222ddad283715e0443899755da41fc014c65a9d6bb6f531fe70  main.js
df5f59d69ae9e4b1a607181b3da7e558fec842c61e9d93774070014632507991  manifest.json
f0e9d8e49ffa83be698ee1ee83fc8e8d314fcea29445853680b6cc64acce1406  styles.css
31e6edb9dd8c9a08318a9a0f9ad7647937dad989271435eeb7d626f299f39ba7  codex-echoink-v0.6.0-card.svg
```

</details>
releases.name: 0.6.0 - Setup guide and knowledge maintenance

releases.author: AKin-lvyifang
releases.description:
![Codex EchoInk v0.5.2](https://github.com/AKin-lvyifang/codex-echoink/releases/download/0.5.2/codex-echoink-0.5.2-card.svg)

## v0.5.2

Knowledge workflow and diagnostics update.

### Same-version update

- In the Knowledge channel, ordinary messages now stay as normal Agent chat.
- Only explicit `/ask`, `/query`, `/ķŚ«`, or `/µ¤źĶ»ó` commands trigger Knowledge Q&A.
- The Knowledge channel primary button now stops ordinary Agent chat when that chat is running, instead of canceling Knowledge maintenance by mistake.
- Knowledge failures now keep more complete app-server, JSON-RPC, OpenCode, and turn error details.
- Local vault note paths and report paths in Knowledge replies render as clickable note links.

### New Features

- Added Knowledge weekly reviews and Agent chat weekly reviews.
- Weekly reviews can run on schedule or manually, and generate Markdown plus HTML in `outputs/obsidian-weekly-review/`.
- Added EchoInk's built-in HTML preview for generated review reports.
- Added `/week` and `/week agent` shortcuts in the Knowledge channel.
- Upgraded `/journal` to write into the current `journal/daily/YYYY-MM/YYYY-MM-DD-Õæ©X.md` layout.
- `/journal` now creates missing journal folders and uses a fixed `00:00` to next-day `06:00` work window.
- OpenCode API mode can collect OpenCode chat history for `/journal`.
- Expanded `/ask` evidence from `wiki/` to `wiki/`, `journal/`, and `outputs/`.
- Added citation buckets, excerpt lines, relevance labels, and match reasons for local Knowledge answers.
- Added a Chinese / English settings language switch.

### Fixes

- Changed the Codex CLI default model to `Auto`.
- Existing saved `gpt-5.5` defaults are migrated to `Auto`.
- Removed remaining hard-coded `gpt-5.5` fallback paths from Knowledge tasks and Plan mode.
- Added detailed diagnostics for WebSocket failures, proxy refusal, missing CLI, timeouts, and app-server exits.
- Added troubleshooting guidance for Windows `responses_websocket` / `os error 10061`.
- Simplified Review settings so manual report generation has confirmation and clearer output paths.
- Codex CLI Knowledge runs now use the normal process timeline, so reasoning, commands, file edits, and final results are easier to inspect.

### Install

Download `codex-echoink-0.5.2.zip`, unzip it, and place the `codex-echoink` folder in:

```text
<vault>/.obsidian/plugins/codex-echoink/
```

### Assets

- `codex-echoink-0.5.2.zip`
- `main.js`
- `manifest.json`
- `styles.css`

SHA256:

```text
7fcda68dd2f4683b7984379956231b0883e038b6e239df48f6137c77a7fd7205  codex-echoink-0.5.2.zip
19953e426224c6b810f039001aa509c7aaa1dd098bf4aabb1d4f8f0c491f99f4  main.js
f06f93be57fed45fc874f583f143c20d9593e42f004018b3f314ad6fee8c043d  manifest.json
5c896e5f1f6a9e0d8237adc5b41655fe64c4507ea8b69f8d8c149b559b919f0c  styles.css
```
releases.name: 0.5.2 - Knowledge workflow and diagnostics

releases.author: AKin-lvyifang
releases.description:
Community review fix.

- Removed the redundant word "Obsidian" from manifest.json description to satisfy automated community checks.
- Rebuilt release assets for version 0.5.1.

Assets:
- main.js
- manifest.json
- styles.css
- codex-echoink-0.5.1.zip
releases.name: 0.5.1 - Community manifest check fix

releases.author: AKin-lvyifang
releases.description:
![Codex EchoInk](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/AKin-lvyifang/codex-echoink/main/docs/images/codex-echoink-knowledge-v0.5.0.png)

## Version

0.5.0

## New features

- Renamed the plugin to Codex EchoInk and prepared the community plugin id `codex-echoink`.
- Added clearer privacy and permission disclosures for Codex CLI, OpenCode, model providers, API keys, and vault write boundaries.
- Kept compatibility with large raw message files from older manual installs under `.obsidian/plugins/obsidian-codex/raw`.
- Added community-ready release assets: `main.js`, `manifest.json`, `styles.css`, and `codex-echoink-0.5.0.zip`.

## How to use

1. Install Codex CLI and log in for Codex CLI mode.
2. Optionally install OpenCode if you want OpenCode API mode for knowledge base tasks.
3. In Obsidian, enable Codex EchoInk and open the EchoInk sidebar.
4. Open the Knowledge channel and start with `/init`, `/check`, or `/ask`.
releases.name: 0.5.0 - Codex EchoInk community ready

releases.description:
![Codex for Obsidian v0.4.0](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/AKin-lvyifang/obsidian-codex/main/docs/images/obsidian-codex-knowledge-v0.4.0.png)

## v0.4.0

This release adds the first Knowledge Base Operations workflow for maintaining an Obsidian vault from inside the Codex sidebar.

## New

- Persistent `Knowledge` channel bound to the current Obsidian vault.
- Chat-first command templates: `/check`, `/maintain`, `/outputs`, `/journal`, `/inbox`.
- WeChat, web page, and file capture entry points for Raw Sources.
- Configurable knowledge base rules file: default `AGENTS.md`, or a custom Markdown file such as `CLAUDE.md`.
- OpenCode API mode model selection.
- OpenCode Agent selection.
- Editor action: translate selected text to English.

## How To Use

1. Download and unzip `obsidian-codex-0.4.0.zip`.
2. Move the `obsidian-codex` folder into `<vault>/.obsidian/plugins/`.
3. Restart Obsidian and enable `Codex for Obsidian`.
4. Open the `Knowledge` channel in the Codex sidebar.
5. Use `/check` for a safe health check, `/maintain` to process new Raw Sources, and `/outputs` to turn outputs into wiki-ready knowledge.
6. In OpenCode API mode, install OpenCode locally, then refresh and choose the model and Agent in settings.

## Safety Boundary

- Existing Raw files are not rewritten.
- Files are not deleted.
- Archiving is not automatic.
- Knowledge tasks write to the configured wiki, outputs, journal, index, and tracker paths.

## Download Check

- Asset: `obsidian-codex-0.4.0.zip`
- SHA256: `154bbce9671070e75d25356d0f290960ce483289012ba034cf39df085bfaedc1`
